goals.title: Lock in first GG-set hold within ₱5K budget
goals.why: GG set is fully out of print. The Regigigas + Cynthia pair is the best out-of-print GG exposure achievable inside the ₱5K budget — buy both for a ~₱4,100 combined long-term hold.

goals.title: Secure best TG buy — Umbreon V (TG06)
goals.why: Umbreon has one of the deepest fan bases in Pokémon and TG06 is the accessible entry vs the ₱30K+ Evolving Skies VMAX alt. Liquidly sellable in PH.

goals.title: Save toward Origin Forme Palkia VSTAR (GG67)
goals.why: Lowest-entry card in the 'actually moving' tier of GG (+12.8% in 30 days). The first stretch goal above ₱5K — build a dedicated ₱8–10K pot, then buy.

goals.title: Hold sealed Chaos Rising to minimum hold dates
goals.why: Discipline goal, not a savings goal. Do NOT crack or sell early. Bundle: hold 6+ mo, sell half at +50%. Blisters: hold 3+ mo. Mega Greninja SIR if pulled — hold 2–3 mo minimum.

goals.title: Add a stable Eeveelution SIR (Sylveon/Espeon)
goals.why: Eeveelution demand is permanent and Prismatic Evolutions is stable post-release. Low-risk slow grower to round out the portfolio once higher-priority buys are done.

holdings.reason: Blue-chip. Charizard TG (TG03) is the strongest card you own — flagged as a PSA grading candidate (raw ~₱2,090 → ₱6,000+ graded). Keep long-term.

Per sealed rules: hold blisters 3+ months, sell trigger +30% (₱1,700–1,900). Watch for reprint news.
Per sealed rules: hold 6+ months, sell half at +50% (₱3,000), hold the rest 12+ months.

holdings.card: Sealed Perfect Order Sleeved Blister Packs
holdings.reason: Per sealed rules: hold 6–18 months. Mega Evo block early sets up 20–40%; target ₱1,700–2,100.

watchlist.name: Giratina VSTAR
watchlist.notes: King of the GG set. Up +64.6% in 30 days as of May 2026. 6th most trending card globally. Way over ₱5K budget but the card to aim for long-term.

watchlist.name: Origin Forme Palkia VSTAR
watchlist.notes: Up +12.8% in 30 days. Lowest-entry card in the 'actually moving' tier of GG. Save to ₱8–10K budget then buy this. Part of the God trio with Dialga and Giratina.

watchlist.name: Regigigas VSTAR
watchlist.notes: Best GG card accessible within ₱5K budget. Out-of-print set. Slow grower but steady. Pair with Cynthia's Ambition for a ~₱4,100 combined GG play.

watchlist.name: Cynthia's Ambition
watchlist.notes: Trainer supporter card. Cynthia is one of the most beloved characters in the franchise — GG trainer cards are underpriced vs Pokémon cards. Sleeper pick. Pairs with Regigigas for ~₱4,100 combined.

watchlist.name: Umbreon V
watchlist.notes: Best TG buy within ₱5K budget. Umbreon has one of the deepest fan bases in Pokémon. Accessible entry point vs the ₱30,000+ Evolving Skies Umbreon VMAX Alt Art. Liquidly sellable in PH.

watchlist.name: Pikachu VMAX
watchlist.notes: The top TG card from Lost Origin. Pikachu + VMAX treatment. Far out of ₱5K range but the TG card to save toward after GG67.

watchlist.name: Pikachu
watchlist.notes: Last card in the TG set — collectors chasing full TG sets need this. Pikachu demand is eternal. Liquidly sellable in PH. Good complement to TG03 Charizard already in portfolio.

watchlist.name: Mega Greninja ex SIR
watchlist.notes: The #1 chase card of 2026 so far. Mega Evo block SIRs have opened at $100+ day-one and hit $400+ within months. If pulled from sealed Chaos Rising product — DO NOT sell immediately. Hold 2–3 months minimum. If buying raw from PH seller, target under ₱6,000.

watchlist.name: Sylveon ex / Espeon ex SIR
watchlist.notes: Eeveelution collector demand is permanent. Prismatic Evolutions is stable post-release. Look for PH sellers with extras at near-market price. Slow grower but low risk.

sealedRules.riskNote: Sell half at +50% (₱3,000). Hold rest 12+ months. Watch for reprint announcements.
Crack one at 3 months if needed. Hold one sealed 12+ months. Sell trigger per blister: ₱1,700–1,900.
Hold 6–18 months. Mega Evo block early sets up 20–40%. Target ₱1,700–2,100.

avoidList: Random Art Rares of non-popular Pokémon (Patrat, Pignite, Yanma, Clamperl, Shedinja, Electrike, Bibarel)
Legacy GX cards from Sun & Moon era — soft market, low growth
Booster bundles as a long-term investment format — worst sealed ROI
Buying sealed at scalper prices right at launch — wait 4–8 weeks for price correction
Any card where you cannot name 3 PH buyers who would want it
buyChecklist: Is the Pokémon one of: Charizard, Greninja, Gardevoir, Gengar, Mewtwo, Pikachu, Eevee line, Lucario, Umbreon, Rayquaza?
Is the rarity GG, TG, SIR, or IR (not common/uncommon)?
Is the set still live or within 12 months of release?
Is the price at or below US market price in PHP equivalent?
Can you name 3 PH buyers who would want this right now?
Do you have a clear exit plan (flip in 3mo / hold 12mo / specific buyer)?
generalNotes: GG set is fully out of print — any GG card is a patient long-term hold
Mega Evolution block (Ascended Heroes, Perfect Order, Chaos Rising) is the hottest block in modern TCG as of mid-2026
PH local prices typically run 5–20% above US market — factor this into cost basis
Sealed sell thresholds: +30% for blisters, +50% for bundles, +100% for ETBs and boxes
If pulled from sealed: never sell a chase SIR immediately — minimum 2–3 month hold
PSA grading Charizard TG03 could turn ₱2,090 raw into ₱6,000+ graded — consider when budget allows
